The unmarshal_object function allows for arbitrary class instantiation and state injection by dynamically importing modules and calling __setstate__ on any class available in the Python environment. An attacker can exploit this by submitting a specially crafted JSON or CBOR payload to an application using these serializers", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0081, "percentile": 0.52549}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-9cfw-f3f9-7mm7", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-04T21:36:43Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cfw-f3f9-7mm7", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-9cfw-f3f9-7mm7"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-31072"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-19T16:16:20Z", "published_at": "2026-05-19T18:32:11Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-31072", "https://gist.github.com/nedlir/11fb77f35a59cbba73392a086b02a9c6", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cfw-f3f9-7mm7"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/agronholm/apscheduler", "summary": "APScheduler's JSONSerializer and CBORSerializer are vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via Insecure Deserialization", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-04T21:36:45Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cfw-f3f9-7mm7", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "apscheduler"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.0.0a1, <= 4.0.0a6"}], "withdrawn_at": null}
